name: GoDrop – S3-Backed File Sharing
raw_facts: Language: Go 1.23 with `net/http` standard library for the web server.
Storage Architecture: Dual-layered storage interface supporting both local filesystem (`FileStorage`) and S3-compatible object storage (`S3Storage`).
Cloudflare R2 Optimization: Configured `aws-sdk-go` with `S3ForcePathStyle: true` and `Region: 'auto'` for seamless R2 compatibility.
Data Integrity: Implemented mandatory SHA-256 content hashing for every upload to enable unique file identification and de-duplication logic.
MIME Management: Developed a custom mapping system to provide precise Content-Type headers for 15+ file formats (PDF, JPEG, PNG, ZIP, etc.) in S3 `PutObject` calls.

Serverless Indexing: Engineered a human-readable 'Code Word' system that utilizes `ListObjectsV2` for fuzzy prefix matching on S3 keys, effectively using the object storage bucket as a zero-latency index without a separate database.
Performance: Implemented memory-buffered uploads using `io.ReadAll` and `bytes.NewReader` to satisfy `io.ReadSeeker` requirements for S3 SDK compliance.
Deployment: Built-in support for `.env` configuration and environment variable injection for 12-factor app compliance.
business_impact: Eliminated user friction by providing a 'no-login' instant file sharing workflow, reducing 'Time-to-Share' to the duration of a single POST request.
Reduced storage costs by leveraging Cloudflare R2's zero egress fees while maintaining enterprise-grade durability.
